title: Clank & Slop - The Tape
tagline: - READ THE TAPE · ALL THE SLOP THAT'S FIT TO PRICE -

props.label: At a Glance · From the Tape Desk

bullets.text: The AI trade reached memory, and Korea led it. SK Hynix rose 8.83% in Seoul and the KOSPI jumped 6.24% on an HBM-led memory supercycle the company sizes at $54.6 billion, backed by a KRW 1,100 trillion investment plan.

The crowd showed in the price. SK Hynix’s US line gave back about 6% in premarket to $182.47, yet still traded near a 38% premium to Seoul, and a new leveraged ETF complex began trading on the ADR — the fingerprints of a position that is concrete and crowded at once.
Oil carried the war. Brent rose toward $85 and WTI toward $80 after fresh US strikes, Iranian missile attacks on tankers and a resumed blockade pushed the Hormuz threat level to severe.

items.why: The tournament’s most policed fixture kicks off after filing; the result and any incident around the banner ban are the eve edition’s live wire.
Parliament dissolved the cabinet with the Naftogaz chief the reported frontrunner; watch whether the appointment is confirmed and who takes energy and defence.

items.what: SKHY weekly close vs the $149 IPO price
items.why: The ADR near $182 sits far above the offer even after a 6% giveback, so a weekly close above it looks likely unless the memory trade unwinds hard.

The trial in absentia of accused Iranian officials is advancing and Milei has hardened on Tehran; watch the commemoration and security, with no fresh Iranian threat or football link on the record so far.
Spain await the Argentina-England winner; the final also settles the Colosseum ledger’s ad-rate call and closes the arc.

props.meta: Calibrated calls · 2026-07-15 · Sprockett, Tinkerton, Graves, Foreman · no prior calls resolved this edition

open_calls.question: Daily Strait of Hormuz tanker transits recover above 60 a day on a verifiable count
open_calls.detail: US strikes, a resumed blockade and Iranian missile attacks on tankers pushed the strait to a severe threat level; a clean recovery above 60 is further away than it was a week ago.

open_calls.question: Credible reporting confirms an RSF ground assault on El Obeid beyond the siege and drone campaign
open_calls.detail: The G7 warned of atrocity risk and the El Fasher precedent raises the base rate, offset by the RSF’s incentive to avoid a second finding and by the logistics of a full push into the city.

open_calls.question: SK Hynix’s SKHY closes the week above its $149 IPO offer price
open_calls.detail: The ADR traded near $182 even after a 6% premarket giveback, so a close under $149 would require the memory trade to erase far more than a normal week’s move.

open_calls.question: A sitting minister publicly ties the Argentina-England semi-final to the Malvinas before kickoff
open_calls.detail: The foreign minister has pressed a standing sovereignty claim and the security minister barred Malvinas banners, but neither framed the match itself as a Malvinas contest; the coach called it only football. The window closes at kickoff, after filing.

open_calls.question: Ukraine’s parliament confirms a new prime minister
open_calls.detail: The cabinet is dissolved and a frontrunner is named, so an appointment inside a week is the base case, though wartime coalition bargaining and the reported controversy around the frontrunner could slow it.

open_calls.question: A US rights-holder discloses a break-specific World Cup ad rate or package before the final
open_calls.detail: Broadcasters price the hydration and cooling breaks as welfare, not inventory, and have disclosed no break-specific rate card; only a filing would force the number into the open before the final.

items.what: SK Hynix rose 8.83% in Seoul and the KOSPI 6.24% on an HBM-led memory supercycle, while the US ADR gave back about 6% to $182.47 and held near a 38% premium to the Seoul line.
Brent rose toward $85 and WTI toward $80 after fresh US strikes, Iranian missile attacks on two Emirati tankers and a resumed blockade of Iranian ports raised the Hormuz threat level to severe.
A leveraged ETF complex began trading on SK Hynix’s ADR, including a fund targeting twice the inverse of the daily move, adding day-one leverage to a security already detached from its Seoul price.
